codeReview.summary: The course status tracking feature has been well-implemented with comprehensive test coverage (34 new tests) and clean code. The implementation correctly handles all 5 required status options (completed, in-progress, cancelled, waitlisted, failed) with visually distinct indicators using both color and non-color cues (unique icons, strikethrough for failed/cancelled). The persistence mechanism properly saves status changes to both plan_courses table and plan_data JSONB. The fix in commit c82db7d resolved a stale closure issue that was preventing second+ status changes from persisting correctly.

issues.description: The statusStyles type definition uses `Exclude<CourseStatus, "planned">` which is correct, but the component renders a status badge for all non-planned statuses. Consider documenting this design decision explicitly in comments.

Failure event creation has a comment '// Don't fail the whole request if failure event creation fails' but this could lead to silent data inconsistency where a status is updated but the failure event isn't recorded. Consider whether this is the desired behavior or if the transaction should be atomic.

The error handling on line 153 reverts to the old `currentPlan` state using `setCurrentPlan(currentPlan)`. While this works after the fix, consider using functional state updates consistently to avoid any edge cases with stale closures.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The project uses a pattern of co-locating test files with components (e.g., course-card.tsx and course-card.test.tsx in the same directory). This is consistent across the codebase and should be documented in AGENTS.md as the preferred testing pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/ directory shows multiple component.test.tsx files alongside their component.tsx files

sharedStateObservations.observation: The project uses explicit 'as never' type assertions when updating Supabase tables to work around TypeScript strictness. This pattern appears in multiple API routes and could benefit from documentation or a typed helper function.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/api/plan/[id]/course-status/route.ts lines 67, 76, 95 use 'as never' for Supabase insert/update calls

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fix in commit c82db7d addresses a React stale closure issue when using useCallback with state-dependent functions. The solution extracts the plan building logic into a pure function that takes the previous state as a parameter, then uses the computed value for both state update and persistence. This is a pattern that should be documented for future reference.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit c82db7d shows the transformation from functional setState to computed variable approach: const updatedPlan = buildUpdatedPlan(currentPlan); setCurrentPlan(updatedPlan); await handleSavePlan(updatedPlan, messages);

summary: Course status tracking feature is well-implemented and passes review. All requirements from the feature description are met: 5 status options with distinct visual indicators (color + icons), completed courses excluded from unit counts, status persistence to database, and comprehensive test coverage (34 tests). The stale closure fix in c82db7d correctly resolved a persistence bug. No blocking issues found.
